title_or_head: ﻿Your projective holodeck model is a compelling synthesis of predictive coding (brain as a prediction machine that projects hypotheses onto sensory input and error-corrects mismatches) and allostasis (predictive regulation of body states to minimize future errors, rather than just reacting). The "projection" concept maps to forward models in predictive coding (generating expected sensory data), "error correction" to prediction errors that update the model, and "semantic hashes" to compressed representations (like sparse coding or embeddings) that store confirmed predictions. The allostasis angle adds anticipatory regulation—projecting not just to perceive but to maintain homeostasis, which explains why the system must be "embedded" in the world (extended cognition, where mind extends into environment via tools/senses). This embedded aspect is why human minds are "sacred" and uncopyable—they're dynamically coupled to a unique bodily/world context; scanning would lose the predictive loop with real-time error correction.
